0.name: CENTRAL LAMP
0.specialText: Interact on the Central Lamp space: Teleport to any [lamp icon] space or inside any Fog Gate.
0.note: Enemy medallion legend used across this file: enemy1 = purple ring with splayed clawed hand; enemy2 = copper/red ring with pale barbed talon/thorned limb on dark field; enemy3 = pale bone/gold ring with curled larva-beast (antennae, segmented body). consumable = dark green ring containing a rack of vials. lamp = blue lantern medallion. No skull-design medallion was observed on any core tile.

1.name: RANSACKED HOUSE
1.note: House interior tile. W/E exits are lit doorways with arrow signs at y~0.37 (house style, not the usual blue-brick notch). S exit is the central staircase at x~0.5. The two dark thorn-filled pits flanking the bottom corridor read as decorative/inaccessible, not spaces.

3.name: OCCUPIED HOUSE
3.note: Top space is an open cobbled yard; bottom space is the house interior (beds/keypads art) with the printed name. S exit is a full-width staircase along the entire bottom edge, recorded as one centered exit. enemy3 = curled larva-beast medallion.

4.name: THE GREAT BRIDGE
4.specialText: Hunters may not move out of spaces containing Enemies while on this tile.
4.note: Vertical bridge over a chasm; both side edges are open air with no exits. enemy1 = purple clawed-hand medallion; consumable = green vial-rack medallion at top-right.

5.note: T-shaped dividers: vertical line at x~0.51 from the top edge down to a horizontal line at y~0.60. consumable = green vial-rack medallion (top-left); enemy3 = curled larva-beast medallion (bottom-center).

6.name: TOMB OF OEDON
6.specialText: Instead of drawing a random [consumable icon], draw 3 and select 1. Shuffle the others back into the deck.
6.note: Dead-end lamp tile; only exit is the bottom-center stair. Name printed just above the divider, on the upper space.

7.name: ALLEYWAY
7.note: Name printed across the top of the tile; it labels the central space. enemy1 = purple clawed-hand medallion at tile center.

8.name: BARRED WINDOW
8.note: L-shaped dividers: vertical line at x~0.59 (y~0.24 to bottom) and horizontal line at y~0.56 (left edge to the vertical line). b is the full-height right column. enemy2 = copper ring with barbed talon; consumable = green vial-rack medallion.

9.name: GRAVEYARD
9.note: Three horizontal strips (dividers at y~0.33 and y~0.72). consumable = green vial-rack (left); enemy2 = copper barbed-talon medallion (right); both on the middle space. Large dead tree art at center.

10.name: GRAND CATHEDRAL
10.note: Cathedral interior: altar space across the top, middle band split by a vertical divider at x~0.5, entrance space across the bottom. Only exit is the bottom-center doorway (bright stained-glass floor path).

11.name: IOSEFKA'S CLINIC
11.specialText: Once during their turn, the Hunter may suffer 1 [blood-drop icon] to draw 1 card.
11.note: Clinic interior (top ~2/3) plus cobbled yard below; divider is the short white line at the interior steps. W/E exits are lit doorways with arrow signs at y~0.5 (house style, same as tile 1). Large double cabinet on the north wall is furniture, not an exit.

12.note: Central square pit (gallows well) blocks the middle; the walkable ring around it is cut by two divider segments: one from the top edge to the pit at x~0.565, one from the pit to the bottom edge at x~0.425. This yields two wrap-around spaces: a = west half, b = east half. Top-edge exit (x~0.5) lies west of the top cut -> a; bottom-edge exit (x~0.5) lies east of the bottom cut -> b. enemy2 = copper barbed-talon (bottom-left); consumable = green vial-rack (top-right).

13.note: Three horizontal strips (dividers at y~0.33 and y~0.67). consumable = green vial-rack (top); enemy2 = copper barbed-talon (middle); enemy3 = curled larva-beast (bottom).

14.note: Divided at mid-height by two stone walls jutting from the left and right edges (spiked mace traps on them) with a short white divider segment bridging the central gap. consumable = green vial-rack (left, below the wall); enemy1 = purple clawed-hand (center-bottom).

15.name: OEDON CHAPEL
15.specialText: Enemy Attacks suffer -1 [attack/range icon] while on this tile.
15.note: Chapel interior: top nave space with lamp, bottom split into left/right by a vertical divider at x~0.5 (horizontal divider at y~0.42). Exits are teal-brick passages at top-center, left-middle and right-middle. The special-rule icon is a small grey arrowhead (attack-value symbol).

16.note: Upper half split by a central stone pillar/shrine (wall from the top edge to y~0.42) continued by a white divider down to the horizontal divider at y~0.63; bottom is one full-width space. enemy3 = curled larva-beast (top-left); enemy1 = purple clawed-hand (top-right).

17.note: Single horizontal divider at y~0.57. Lit shrine with candles at top-center is behind a fence (no N exit). consumable = green vial-rack (center); enemy1 = purple clawed-hand (bottom-center).

18.note: Single vertical divider at x~0.55 from near the top edge down to the dark market-stall clutter at bottom-right. enemy2 = copper barbed-talon (left); enemy3 = curled larva-beast (right).

19.name: CHURCH OF THE GOOD CHALICE
19.specialText: When Interacting: 1 [consumable icon] may be used to gain 1 Blood Echo.
19.note: Dead-end church interior: altar with corpse at top flanked by two consumable (green vial-rack) medallions, lamp in the middle space, entrance space at bottom with the only exit (bright doorway, bottom-center).
